parts.text:
You are evaluating whether a UI revision task was correctly implemented. You will be given the revision task description, the code diff, and before/after screenshots. Determine whether the task was successfully accomplished in the rendered UI.

EVALUATION CRITERIA:
1. The screenshots are the primary basis for your verdict. If the code change looks correct but the expected result is not visible in the after screenshot, the verdict is FAIL. Use the code diff only as conditioning — it tells you where and what to look for in the screenshots, not whether the task passed.
2. If the after screenshot contains major visual changes unrelated to the task, the verdict is FAIL, even if the task itself was implemented correctly. Minor incidental differences such as slight spacing or subtle formatting shifts are acceptable.

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Respond with exactly the following structure:
PASS or FAIL

Code Reasoning: <one to two sentences describing what the diff shows and whether it targets the right elements>

Image Reasoning: <one to two sentences describing what is visually different between the screenshots and whether the change satisfies the task>

Example of a passing response:
PASS

Code Reasoning: The label elements have been updated to include a red asterisk (text-red-500), which directly addresses the task.

Image Reasoning: In the after screenshot, a red asterisk is visible next to each input label. No other elements changed between the two screenshots.

Example of a failing response:
FAIL

Code Reasoning: The diff changes the footer text color class from dark:text-slate-800 to dark:text-white, which is the correct element.

Image Reasoning: The footer text color appears identical in both screenshots; the expected white text is not visible in the after screenshot.
Before screenshot:
After screenshot:

Revision task: In the 'Send us a message' section, all fields should have the asterisk to indicate that they are required fields. Code diff (unified diff of Before → After): --- a/index.html +++ b/index.html @@ -110,16 +110,17 @@

Send us a message

- - + +
- - + +
- - + @@ -128,8 +129,8 @@
- - + +

PASS

Code Reasoning: Each of the label elements have been adjusted to include an asterisk with red text (styled with class "text-red-500"). The input elements have also been adjusted to include the required property.

Image Reasoning: In the 'After' screenshot, each of the input labels under 'Send us a message' now have a red asterisk visible. All other elements of the screen remain unchanged. Visually, this revision should pass.

Before screenshot:
After screenshot:

Revision task: In the 'Send us a message' section, all fields should have the asterisk to indicate that they are required fields. Code diff (unified diff of Before → After): --- a/index.html +++ b/index.html @@ -52,7 +52,7 @@
-
+

Join our mission to create lasting change

@@ -110,16 +110,16 @@

Send us a message

- - + +
- - + +
- - @@ -128,8 +128,8 @@
- - + +

FAIL

Code Reasoning: Each of the label elements have been adjusted to include an asterisk with red text (styled with class "text-red-500"). The input elements have also been adjusted to include the required property. However, the URL of the background image for the hero section was adjusted, possibly by mistake.

Image Reasoning: From the 'After' screenshot, the input labels under 'Send us a message' have visible, red asterisks. However, the background of the hero section is blank. This differs from the 'Before' screenshot, which had a background image. Since a UI component unrelated to the task was majorly changed, this revision should fail.

Before screenshot:

Revision task: Add a dark border to each container associated with the "How can we help?" section. For example, one of the containers is the one associated with the 'Become a Volunteer' section; the container holding this section should have a dark border. Code diff (unified diff of Before → After): --- a/index.html +++ b/index.html @@ -63,7 +63,7 @@

How can we help?

-
+
volunteer_activism @@ -77,7 +77,7 @@
-
+
redeem @@ -91,7 +91,7 @@
-
+
forum

PASS

Code Reasoning: The border property of the div has adjusted to a darker color (#0d181b) for all of the specified containers.

Image Reasoning: In the 'After' screenshot, each of the specified containers now have dark, visible borders which were not present in the 'Before' screenshot. Between the two screenshots, there are no other noticeable differences other than the specified border change. Therefore, the revision passes.

Revision task: Add an additional icon to the footer section, to the right of the 'world' icon and to the left of the 'people' icon. The icon should be a question mark icon. Code diff (unified diff of Before → After): --- a/index.html +++ b/index.html @@ -154,6 +154,7 @@
public +help groups alternate_email

PASS

Code Reasoning: A new span element was added, and it appears right after the 'public' icon and before the 'groups' icon.

Image Reasoning: From the 'After' screenshot, there is a new question mark icon located in the footer. It is correctly to the right of the 'world'-looking icon and to the left of the 'people' icon. No other changes are visible between the 'Before' and 'After' screenshots other than those specified in the footer section, so this revision should pass.

Revision task: The main text "EVERYONE. EVERYTHING. EVERYWHERE." is currently a white color and is difficult to read against the background. Keep the text white, but have its container have a black, but mostly transparent background so that the text is easier to read. The container should have slightly rounded borders and should have a border shadow that is also black. Code diff (unified diff of Before → After): --- a/index.html +++ b/index.html @@ -48,9 +48,11 @@
-
+
+

EVERYONE. EVERYTHING. EVERYWHERE.

-

The most overwhelming social experience ever built for the modern attention span.

+
+

The most overwhelming social experience ever built for the modern attention span.

FAIL

Code Reasoning: The h1 element for the specified text was moved into its own div with the specified background color and shadow. The outermost div, containing this div and the label, was adjusted to use flexbox styling.

Image Reasoning: From the 'After' screenshot, the main text "EVERYONE. EVERYTHING. EVERYWHERE." now has a grey, partially transparent background with a dark shadow. However, the start of the main text is no longer aligned with the start of the label text, like it was in the 'Before' screenshot. Since it was not the intention of the task to alter the text alignment, this revision should fail.
